name: Ace of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Seeds in fertile ground. The Ace of Pentacles is a financial or material opportunity — the first coin dropped into the account, the land earned, the business begun.
reversedMeaning: Lost opportunity, lack of planning, scarcity mindset. The seed falls on barren ground — or the opportunity is squandered before it can grow.
symbolism: single pentacle emerging from a cloud

name: Two of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The art of keeping balance. Two of Pentacles depicts juggling at the edge of capability — multiple priorities, changing demands, the graceful dance of adaptive management.
reversedMeaning: Overwhelm, disorganization, missed deadlines. The balls drop — or the balance was always an illusion masking chaos underneath.
symbolism: figure dancing with two pentacles on a figure-8 rope

name: Three of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The craftsman's craft. Three of Pentacles shows mastery in the making — skilled hands working together, the craftsman consulting the plan, a project that rewards patient collaboration.
reversedMeaning: Lack of teamwork, poor quality, working alone. The collaboration breaks down — or the work is done without the blueprint that would make it excellent.
symbolism: architects and stonemasons at work on a cathedral

name: Four of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The keeper of coins. Four of Pentacles depicts tight-fisted holding — security sought through accumulation, stability purchased through control, the miser who has everything and enjoys nothing.
reversedMeaning: Greed, materialism, letting go of control. The grip loosens — or the control becomes the cage that keeps abundance out.
symbolism: richly robed figure clutching a pentacle with one foot

name: Five of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Darkness visible. Five of Pentacles shows a figure walking past a stained glass window — material and spiritual poverty, the church in sight but not entered, worry that blinds to help available.
reversedMeaning: Recovery from loss, spiritual poverty, finding help. The light is seen at last — or accepting that enough is enough and asking for help.
symbolism: figure with cloak passing a church with five windows
the church visible but not entered

name: Six of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Balance in exchange. Six of Pentacles depicts giving and receiving in equilibrium — charity that empowers rather than patronizes, the joy of balanced generosity from those who have to those who need.
reversedMeaning: Debt, strings attached, inequity. The gift has hidden conditions — or the imbalance is deliberate, power hoarded rather than shared.
symbolism: rich figure giving coins to two beggars
scales of justice in one hand

name: Seven of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The long game. Seven of Pentacles shows a figure leaning on their staff, looking at seeds planted but not yet harvested — patience required, the work has been done, the return is not yet visible.
reversedMeaning: Impatience, poor returns, wasted effort. The harvest does not come — or the waiting is extended indefinitely while the ground grows barren.
symbolism: figure leaning on staff examining a pentacle-laden vine

name: Eight of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The dedicated craftsman. Eight of Pentacles depicts mastery in progress — hours of focused practice, skills refined under a master, the quiet dignity of work done well for its own sake.
reversedMeaning: Obsession with status, perfectionism, lack of growth. The craft becomes a prison — or mastery is mistaken for the work itself and joy is lost.
symbolism: carver of pentacles at a workbench
five pentacles in various stages of completion

name: Nine of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The good life earned. Nine of Pentacles shows a figure at ease in cultivated abundance — comfort purchased through discipline, beauty that rewards the eye and the soul, independence that was built rather than inherited.
reversedMeaning: Overindulgence, superficiality, living beyond means, envy. The comfort becomes a prison — or the appearance of wealth masks the absence of substance.
symbolism: gracious figure with pentacle and rose

name: Ten of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The wealth that endures. Ten of Pentacles depicts generational abundance — a family estate, inherited prosperity, the financial foundation that gives the next generation more options than the last.
reversedMeaning: Family conflict, financial failure, instability, break with tradition. The estate crumbles — or the inheritance is a burden that fractures rather than binds the family.
symbolism: old couple and child with dog in front of a manor house
harvest and coins on the ground

name: Page of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The ambitious student of wealth. The Page of Pentacles arrives bearing a vision of prosperity — a plan, an opportunity, a desire to build something that lasts, the first step toward material mastery.
reversedMeaning: Lack of progress, missed opportunities, get-rich-quick schemes. The ambition outpaces the discipline — or the plan never leaves the page where it was written.
symbolism: youthful figure presenting a pentacle at a desk
rural landscape and town in background

name: Knight of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The steady builder. The Knight of Pentacles moves with patient determination — slow, reliable, methodical progress, the long campaign of consistent effort that produces reliable results.
reversedMeaning: Boredom, stagnation, perfectionism, resistance to change. The slowness calcifies into rigidity — or the patience becomes paralysis when action is required.
symbolism: knight on a sturdy horse holding a pentacle

name: Queen of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: The earth mother made manifest. The Queen of Pentacles rules the material realm with nurturing practicality — abundant growth, wise management of resources, a sanctuary created through patient care.
reversedMeaning: Financial insecurity, smothering, materialism, self-neglect. The nurturing has turned to suffocation — or the self was neglected while caring for everything else.
symbolism: throned queen with pentacle and growing plant

name: King of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Master of the material realm. The King of Pentacles commands through accumulation and wise stewardship — wealth built and protected, resources managed with discipline, the empire of the practical mind.
reversedMeaning: Greed, materialism, poor financial decisions, stubbornness. The empire crumbles because the king could not adapt — or wealth hoarded so tightly it becomes worthless.
symbolism: enthroned king with pentacle and bull
